mechanism_narrative: A decrease in the federal funds rate over the prior three months reduces equity discount rates and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive on average during FEDFUNDS decline episodes over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that increases wage cost pressures and compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes yield positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales reflect robust aggregate demand without inventory accumulation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint increases in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to produce positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index accelerates expectations of monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPI increases in high inflation regimes do not produce negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing supply capacity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with rising INDPRO show non-positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 1500 thousand units coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in payroll employment and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening that boosts household income and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Simultaneous PAYEMS growth and UNRATE declines fail to generate positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding one percent signals stronger-than-expected aggregate demand, causing equity markets to re-rate upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises more than 1.0% from prior quarter
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth exceeding 1.0% coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
